meta.title: Joch — The control plane for AI agent fleets
meta.description: Joch is the vendor-neutral control plane for governing, securing, and operating AI agent fleets across every SDK, model, tool, and runtime.

hero.title: Harness your agent fleet.
hero.subtitle: Joch is the vendor-neutral control plane for securing, governing, and operating AI agents across every model, framework, tool, and runtime.

problem.kicker: The questions vendor SDKs leave to the operator
items: What agents exist across my company, and who owns them?
What models, tools, MCP servers, and memory does each agent depend on?
What did each agent do yesterday — and what did it cost?
Can I move an agent from OpenAI to Claude — and what would break?
Can I block all agents from calling email.send without approval?
Can I deploy the same agent locally, in Docker, and in Kubernetes?
Can I compare two agent versions before promoting to production?
Which MCP servers are pinned, and which were quarantined this week?
problem.footnote: If you operate ten, a hundred, or a thousand agents — you need a control plane.

pillars.title: Run agents like production infrastructure.
pillars.subtitle: Joch organizes everything you need to operate AI agents safely — from the inventory layer down to release management.

items.name: Inventory
items.summary: A single system of record for every agent, model, tool, MCP server, memory store, and owner — regardless of which SDK built them.

items.name: Governance
items.summary: Portable policy-as-code for tool use, data access, approvals, model choice, and budgets — enforced uniformly across SDKs.

items.name: Portability
items.summary: One agent record runs locally, in Docker, on Kubernetes, or via managed runtimes; conversation state migrates across providers.

items.name: Observability
items.summary: Every model call, tool call, memory write, RAG retrieval, and approval is captured as an event — exported to OpenTelemetry and OCSF.

items.name: Release Management
items.summary: Version, eval, diff, promote, and roll back agents like production software — with quality gates that block regressions.

products.title: One control plane. Six surfaces.
products.subtitle: Inspect, govern, distribute, and operate your fleet from open-source tools, hosted SaaS, or self-hosted enterprise.

items.name: Joch Console
items.summary: Open-source web console for inventory, traces, approvals, AgBOM, and cost views.

items.name: Joch Registry
items.summary: Open registry of reusable agent templates, policies, and tool / MCP definitions.

items.name: Joch Operator
items.summary: Kubernetes operator that reconciles agents, policies, and traces as first-class resources.

items.name: Joch Cloud
items.summary: Hosted multi-tenant control plane with SSO, RBAC, retention, and SLA — your runtime, our control plane.

items.name: Joch Enterprise
items.summary: Self-hosted enterprise distribution with airgap installer, hardened images, and FedRAMP-ready packaging.

items.name: Joch Marketplace
items.summary: Vetted MCP servers and tools with signing, security review, and SLAs.

integrations.kicker: Bring any agent. Run it anywhere.
integrations.title: Vendor-neutral by design.
integrations.subtitle: Joch sits above your SDKs and providers. Switch frameworks or models without rewriting governance.

items.name: Fleet Inventory
items.summary: Discover, register, and catalog every agent across SDKs and teams in one place.

items.name: MCP Governance
items.summary: Pin, scan, sandbox, and quarantine MCP servers across the entire fleet.

items.name: Cross-Provider Migration
items.summary: Move a live conversation between providers with a deterministic checkpoint and capability check.

items.name: Cost Control
items.summary: Cap, attribute, and alert on agent costs across teams and providers — without changing agent code.

items.name: Release Gates
items.summary: Block agent promotions on failing evals, AgBOM regressions, or missing approvals.

items.name: Approvals
items.summary: Route human approvals for risky tool calls without blocking the agent loop.

pricing.title: Open-core. Honest pricing.
pricing.subtitle: Pay for control-plane operations, never for inference. The OSS core is sufficient for production fleets.

tiers.name: Open Source
tiers.summary: Full control plane, gateways, AOS exporters, CLI, console — self-hostable end to end.

tiers.name: Cloud Starter
tiers.summary: Hosted control plane to remove the hosting friction. Limits on agents, executions, and retention.

tiers.name: Cloud Team
tiers.summary: The typical landing tier for engineering organizations.

tiers.name: Cloud Enterprise
tiers.summary: For regulated industries and large fleets where dedicated hosting matters.
features: Dedicated control plane in your cloud

pricing.footnoteBody: Joch Enterprise ships every Cloud feature in an airgap-friendly distribution with hardened images, FedRAMP packaging, and a dedicated CVE channel.

cta.title: Govern every agent. Audit every action.
cta.subtitle: Install Joch in front of your fleet. Discover, govern, trace, approve, deploy, roll back.

shared.finalCtaSubtitle: Install the open-source core or start on Cloud — same control plane, same audit trail.
shared.metaSuffix: Joch — the control plane for AI agent fleets

console.name: Joch Console
console.tagline: See every agent. Approve every action.
console.summary: Joch Console is the open-source web UI on top of the Joch control plane. It is the glass pane your operators sit in front of when they need to know what an agent did, what it cost, and what it touched.

features.name: Inventory view
features.summary: Every agent across every SDK in one searchable list. Filter by owner, framework, model, MCP server, or runtime.

features.name: Trace explorer
features.summary: Replay an execution end-to-end: every model call, tool call, RAG retrieval, memory write, and approval — with cost attribution per span.

features.name: Approval queue
features.summary: Pending tool calls and policy overrides land here; approve, deny, or modify with reason capture and audit.

features.name: AgBOM viewer
features.summary: Inspect the CycloneDX-extended AgBOM for any agent — models, tools, MCP servers, knowledge sources, and their versions.

includes: Inventory, trace, approval, AgBOM, and cost dashboards
Live SSE updates when state changes
RBAC with org / team / agent scoping
Export to OpenTelemetry, OCSF, CycloneDX, SPDX
Browser themes (dark default) and full keyboard shortcuts

registry.name: Joch Registry
registry.tagline: A registry your agents can pull from.
registry.summary: Joch Registry is the open registry for reusable agent templates, policy bundles, tool definitions, and MCP server descriptors. Versioned, signed, and pullable from any Joch installation.

features.name: Template catalog
features.summary: Publish reusable agent definitions with input/output schemas, default policies, and recommended models.

features.name: Policy library
features.summary: Distribute policy bundles (allowlists, redaction rules, budget caps) as versioned artifacts your fleet pulls in.

features.name: MCP & tool descriptors
features.summary: Pinned, signed MCP server entries with capability declarations and security review status.

features.name: Versioning & signing
features.summary: Semver, content-addressed digests, optional cosign signatures — your agents only pull what is verified.

includes: Search by capability, tag, or owner

operator.name: Joch Operator
operator.summary: Joch Operator turns the Joch resource model into Kubernetes CRDs. Reconcile agents, models, model routes, MCP servers, policies, and traces alongside the rest of your platform — through kubectl, Argo CD, or Flux.

features.name: First-class CRDs
features.summary: Agent, Model, ModelRoute, Tool, MCPServer, Policy, Approval, Trace, AgBOM and the rest of the catalog as native Kubernetes objects.

features.name: Reconciliation loops
features.summary: Continuous controllers keep declared state in sync — restart-safe, GitOps-friendly, idempotent.

features.name: Admission webhooks
features.summary: Block bad policy, drift, or untrusted MCP servers at apply time before they reach the cluster.

features.name: Helm + Kustomize
features.summary: Ship via Helm chart or Kustomize overlay; pin versions and namespaces per team.

includes: Compatible with Argo CD, Flux, Backstage

cli.out: 12 agents · all Reconciled · last sync 4s ago
Conditions: Ready, Synced · Trace 143 events · Last AgBOM v0.7

cloud.name: Joch Cloud
cloud.summary: Joch Cloud is the hosted, multi-tenant control plane. The control plane lives with us; your agents and runtimes stay where they are. Wired up over a customer-runtime tunnel so no inbound port is required.

features.name: Hosted control plane
features.summary: Multi-region, multi-tenant. SSO, SCIM, RBAC. Audit export to your SIEM. SLA-backed.

features.name: Customer-runtime tunnel
features.summary: Outbound-only WebSocket tunnel from your environment — no inbound ports, no firewall changes.

features.name: Managed retention
features.summary: Trace and AgBOM retention up to 90 days on Team, custom on Enterprise. Export anytime.

features.name: Approval routing
features.summary: Slack and email approval routing with reply-to-approve, mention-to-deny, full audit.

includes: Audit export to SIEM (S3, Splunk, Datadog, Elastic)

cli.out: Logged in as you@team-acme.com · org acme · region eu-west-1
Tunnel up · 14ms RTT · agents now reachable from console

enterprise.name: Joch Enterprise
enterprise.summary: Joch Enterprise is the self-hosted distribution for organizations that cannot run on SaaS — regulated industries, airgap environments, FedRAMP customers. Same surfaces as Joch Cloud, packaged for your runtime.

features.name: Airgap installer
features.summary: Single-bundle installer with all images and Helm charts pre-packaged. Works behind proxy with no outbound network.

features.name: Hardened images
features.summary: Distroless base, signed with cosign, SBOM published, CIS-benchmark aligned.

features.name: FedRAMP packaging
features.summary: FIPS-validated cryptography, SSP documentation, control mapping, dedicated CVE channel.

features.name: Advanced policy packs
features.summary: PII redaction, GDPR, HIPAA, ITAR, FedRAMP control maps shipped as policy bundles.

cli.out: Verified signature · loading 24 images · charts ready
Plan: 0 breaking · 3 image upgrades · 2 schema migrations

marketplace.name: Joch Marketplace
marketplace.summary: Joch Marketplace is the curated catalog of MCP servers and tools that your agents can pull. Every entry has a security review, signed artifacts, an SLA, and a clear capability declaration — so you stop discovering tools by name in a wiki.

features.name: Security review
features.summary: Every server reviewed for capability scope, network exposure, supply chain, and known CVEs before listing.

features.name: Signed artifacts
features.summary: Cosign signatures and content-addressed digests; your registry mirror only pulls verified bytes.

features.name: Capability declarations
features.summary: Each entry declares which tools it exposes, which scopes it needs, and what side effects it can produce.

features.name: SLA-backed listings
features.summary: Marketplace partners commit to availability and response targets — escalation paths are part of the contract.

hero.title: Open-core. Honest pricing.
hero.subtitle: The Apache-2.0 core is sufficient for production fleets. Pay for control-plane operations, never for inference.

items.name: Pay for control, not inference
items.summary: Inference billing stays with the provider. Joch is a multiplier on operations efficiency.

items.name: Charge for agents and executions
items.summary: Per-seat undercounts platforms with many automations and few humans. We meter the work.

items.name: No paywalls in OSS
items.summary: Basic AgBOM, traces, hooks, gateways, and policies are free forever in the open-source core.

items.name: Retention is metered
items.summary: Trace and AgBOM storage is a real cost — we charge for it explicitly, not by feature gate.

addOns.name: Premium MCP / Tool Marketplace
addOns.summary: Vetted MCP servers and tools with signing and security review.

addOns.name: Compliance pack
addOns.summary: SOC 2, HIPAA, FedRAMP exports and control mapping.

addOns.name: Custom Guardian Agent
addOns.summary: Bespoke Guardian Agent integration for your tool gateway.

addOns.name: Private Registry mirror
addOns.summary: Joch Registry mirror with private repositories and replication.

faq.q: Is the open-source tier really enough for production?
faq.a: Yes — control plane, gateways, AOS exporters, CLI, console, framework adapters, provider adapters, and the Kubernetes operator are all in the Apache-2.0 distribution. Self-hosted production fleets run on it with no commercial license.

faq.q: What counts as an 'agent' for billing?
faq.a: An agent is one record in the Joch inventory — one named, owned, versioned definition. Different versions of the same agent count as one.
Cloud Team includes a generous monthly execution allowance per agent, then meters overage in tiers. The OSS and Cloud Starter tiers do not meter executions at all.

faq.q: Can I move from Cloud to self-hosted (or back)?
faq.a: Yes. Joch is portable by design — agent records, policies, and traces export and re-import cleanly. Cloud and Enterprise speak the same control plane.
Never. Provider tokens are billed by the provider. Joch routes the call and records the trace; the bytes flow direct.
